description: A JSONB containment or existence query runs without a gin index, forcing a full scan that worsens as data grows.
body: Failure mode: a query filters on data @> '{...}' or data->>'key' = $1 but the only index is a btree on the whole jsonb column (or none), so Postgres scans every row and parses each document. Detection signals: EXPLAIN shows Seq Scan with a jsonb filter; latency climbs with table size; no gin or expression index exists for the operator used. Do not rely on a btree over the jsonb column for containment. Safer fallback: add a gin index (jsonb_path_ops for @>) or an expression index on the extracted scalar field, and confirm EXPLAIN now uses it.
